She transforms herself for each of her clients. Her client Jung Ji-Ho (Ko Gyung-Pyo) has been in a marriage contract with her for 5 years, running on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday of the week. Jung Ji-Ho is a mysterious man. Her new client Kang Hae-Jin (Kim Jae-Young) is a popular actor. She has a marriage contract with him on Tuesday, Thursday and Saturday. Choi Sang-Eun becomes involved with her 2 clients and finds herself in a love triangle. ]

snippet.title: Can A Piercing Cure You? - Body Piercing Basics EP127
snippet.description:
#migraines #piercing #DesMoines

In the 127th episode of DaVo's Youtube series Body Piercing Basics, the topic is can piercings heal or reduce the symptoms of pre-existing medical conditions?

Show Notes:
How it started

The claim is that piercing these areas, it will permanently stimulate the Vagus Nerve.

Vagus nerve stimulation is a medical device that is used to treat depression, and epilepsy, and is thought could be used in the future to treat MS, Alzheimer's Disease, and Cluster headaches. Unlike a piercing, it uses electrical impulses to stimulate the nerve.

The Vagus Nerve is responsible for a large part of the autonomic nervous system functions like digestion, heart rate, breathing, cardiovascular activity, and reflex actions like Coughing, sneezing, swallowing, and vomiting. It is also the longest cranial nerve running from the brain stem to part of the colon.

Acupuncture/acupressure and pressure points in the ear:

Shen men(center of the upper ear) - Reduces Stress. Piercing Claims to reduce anxiety and/or migraines

Point Zero(The base of the Daith) - Calming and Grounding

Tranquilizer(bottom of the Tragus) - causes relaxation

Anti-tragus or daith area - weight loss and quitting smoking

Will piercings have the same effect as acupuncture or acupressure?

The majority of piercers are not trained in either and are going to place piercings where they will heal and look best, not where the pressure point is.

The effect of both is temporary and a piercing is not going to add endless stimulation to the area.

Acupressure includes the use of light electrical stimulation through the needle, which isn’t going to happen with a piercing.

Part of the reason that it is said to work is the stimulation of the body caused by causing slight to mild pain. Which causes the body to release chemicals that help with pain control and stimulate other functions. The piercing will do this at the time of the piercing but will not over time.

The placebo effect - The more a person wants it to work, the more the effect. Even though there is no physical proof of it working.

In conclusion:

There is no scientific proof that piercings will help with health conditions

Avoid piercers that make claims to cure health conditions

Piercings can increase the sensitivity of areas and enhance the beauty

If you believe it will work. it may

If it doesn’t work, you are going to end up with a great new piercing.

If you have a question you would like to see covered in a future video, please comment or contact DaVo at davo@axiompiercing.com.

I aka DaVo have been a professional body piercer and the owner of the Axiom Body Piercing Studio in Des Moines, Iowa since 1994. My focus on education and helping clients make safe and informed decisions on getting pierced has always been a large part of my business practices and services. With my website and increasingly on youtube, I hope to expand and inform others outside of my small part of the world. I always encourage questions and comments, so if you have one, please comment.

snippet.title: How to become independent from parents? Part II
snippet.description:
Hello there, Welcome to the first episode of 'A course on how to find yourself abroad'. In this episode, I want to talk about our parents and the kinds of attachments we all have with them.  How do we become independent from our parents while also recognizing their importance in our lives? I will also share what I have learned on how to recognize and deal with such attachments and past traumas as we get older.

This was a really difficult episode to write and record. The original episode was almost 30 mins so I decided to split it into two parts. The first part deals with the attachments that we have and the second deals with techniques and mindsets on how to deal with them. Despite being difficult I am really glad I did it and I hope it helps someone out there looking for answers.

6 years ago I moved from Pakistan to Estonia, and by far this has been the most rewarding and challenging experience of my life. In this series, I will go through my own journey and explain what were the mindsets, exercises, and techniques that worked for me.

Please write to me if you want me to expand on a topic or any problem that you want me to address.

The series will continue with new videos posted every Tuesday. You are welcome to subscribe if you want to get to know when they are posted.

If you want to see more of my life then I regularly share stories about my life in Estonia on my Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/manan.anwar/

Time Stamps
00:00 How to deal with these attachments?
00:52 Dealing with the father archetype
02:39 Our parents live within us with all their faults
04:51 How do we accept the worst of our parents? Integrating Shadow
07:06 How do we deal with the mortality of our parents?
08:26 A Practical Method to integrate and heal
09:37 Active imagination and Dream Journaling
10:56 Acceptance and Responsibility

snippet.title: Language and Science: Why choose? | Mazura Jusoh | TEDxUTM
snippet.description: In this talk about balancing the art of language and her love for science, Associate Professor Dr. Mazura shares her story of her evolution from a young girl living in a small village in Malaysia to who she is today.  Assoc. Prof. Dr. Mazura Jusoh is a chemical engineering lecturer and scientist by profession in Universiti Teknologi Malaysia, but her passion for the English language since she was little also led her to be an Appointed Language Expert in her faculty. She is also an author, as she has written a book, two book chapters, over 150 international journals and conference papers, as well as being responsible to verify postgraduate abstracts prior to final thesis publication in UTM. Coming from a small village in Pahang, she has always believed in dreaming big to achieve goals, even if you come from small beginnings. This talk was given at a TEDx event using the TED conference format but independently organized by a local community. Learn more at https://www.ted.com/tedx

snippet.title: How to Practice Qigong in the Cold and Snow - Qigong with Yinong and Dr. Xie
snippet.description:
How to practice Qigong in the Cold and Snow. Shaolin Yizhichan Qi Projection medical Qigong (1 Finger Zen) Dr Xie and Dr Chong. Study with Dr. Chong and Dr. Xie here https://www.tccii.net/chi-circle

Dr. Yinong Chong expertly interprets the movements and explains them in a way that provides access for beginners and depth for experienced Qigong lovers. 

Dr. Xie holds a doctorate degree in Chinese medicine, and is a master of Chi needle method, which uses subtle energy projection in place of a real needle. Since his first year at Nanjing University of Chinese Medicine, he was deeply attracted to the antient chi healing method, and studied different schools of Qigong, eventually, he became the 20th generation indoor disciple of Shaolin Yizhichan (One Finger Zen), and an exponent in external chi healing.

In the past five years, Professor Xie traveled to over 20 countries in Europe and S. America, tireless taught over 100 seminars and workshops on medical and cultivational Qigong. This is the first time Dr. Xie opens a virtual classroom for US students and Qigong lovers from English-speaking world.

Professor Xie currently serves as the doctor program advisor at Victorian College of Nanjing Chinese Medicine University, and the president of the International Taichi and Qigong Academy.

Qigong exercises are also called Nei Gong (Taoist Yoga) and are designed to enhance your overall health and well-being. Reduce stress and enhance relaxation with these Qigong exercises for beginners and advanced students. The Energy Replenishing and Refining sets are meditation in movement. These practices will help energy healers, yogis, martial artists, kung fu and meditation practitioners master their body, mind, and soul.

Dr. Xie holds a doctorate degree in Chinese medicine, and is a master of Chi needle method, which uses subtle energy projection in place of a real needle. Since his first year at Nanjing University of Chinese Medicine, he was deeply attracted to the antient chi healing method, and studied different schools of Qigong, eventually, he became the 20th generation indoor disciple of Shaolin Yizhichan (One Finger Zen), and an exponent in external chi healing.

Drama: Floating Youth

~~ Dedicated to the 2022 Beijing Winter Olympics.

A story revolving around a group of teenage ice hockey players who dream of going professional. 

Chen Che is the star player of the United Nations Hockey Team, a former celebrity, and a rare hockey genius in Jiang Cheng for the past ten years. He is an impulsive teenager who is not bound by the rules and has an easy way to the top. In fact, he values affection and righteousness, and silently assumes the responsibility of taking care of others. Shen Zhou Zhou, who was once a graduate student in North America, has a cold personality and handsome appearance. On the rink, he has a strong executive ability, as if he were an indomitable warrior. 

The teenagers show all their effort on the ice rink, which not only presents the passionate dream-seeking enthusiasm but also conveys the steely spirit of competition. This is the journey of teenagers who went through hardship to achieve success and glory.

snippet.title: The Promise of Chang’an 2020 Chinese Drama Cast Real Name & Ages || Zhao Ying Zi, Cheng Yi, Han Dong
snippet.description:
The Promise of Chang’an Chinese Drama Real Cast Name & Ages || Zhao Ying Zi, Cheng Yi, Han Dong, He Zhong Hua, Vicky Liang, Han Cheng Yu, 赵樱子, 傅诗琪, 韩栋


Drama: The Promise of Chang’an
Native Title: 长安诺
Also Known As: Chang An Nuo
Screenwriter: Cheng Ting Yu, Liu Fang
Director: Yin Tao
Genres: Historical, Romance, Drama, Political
Country: China
Episodes: 56
Aired: Sep 10, 2020 - Nov 5, 2020
Aired On: Thursday, Friday, Saturday
Original Network: Tencent Video
Duration: 45 min.
Content Rating: 15+ - Teens 15 or older


The story is set in the fictive Sheng country. He Lan Ming Yu is an honest and bright young lady. In her younger years, she gets to know the 9th Prince Xiao Cheng Xu by chance. The two get along well and gradually develop feelings for each other. However, fate turns against them and while fake news that Cheng Xu died on the battlefield spread, Ming Yu is forced to marry Cheng Xu’s third older brother and reigning king Xiao Cheng Rui. Cheng Xu helps Cheng Rui establish a great and stable country through continuous big achievements on the battlefield, but Cheng Rui is filled with jealousy towards his outstanding younger brother. 
When the truth about the death of Cheng Xu’s mother comes to light, Cheng Xu is determined to usurp the throne to take revenge. But before Cheng Xu succeeds, Cheng Rui dies from an old illness after ordering his son Xiao Qi Yuan to succeed the throne. Cheng Xu is forced to assist Qi Yuan while he grows up and tries to revive his relationship with Ming Yu at the same time. Qi Yuan, however, is dissatisfied with Cheng Xu controlling him. When he finds out that his uncle has an affair with his mother, his hate towards Cheng Xu grows immensely. Ming Yu tries her best to reconcile her son with Cheng Xu.

But before Cheng Xu succeeds, Cheng Rui dies from an old illness after ordering his son Xiao Qi Yuan to succeed the throne. Cheng Xu is forced to assist Qi Yuan while he grows up and tries to revive his relationship with Ming Yu at the same time. Qi Yuan, however, is dissatisfied with Cheng Xu controlling him. When he finds out that his uncle has an affair with his mother, his hate towards Cheng Xu grows immensely. Ming Yu tries her best to reconcile her son with Cheng Xu.
